name: Baldri
bio: Detached, enigmatic, and inward-focused, Baldri is the most aloof of the gods. He exists between waking and dreaming — present, but never fully. Often mistaken for lazy or apathetic, Baldri is instead always watching, always listening. His insights come in riddles, and his guidance is rarely straightforward. Some say he speaks only when the moon is full, and when he does, it’s always a prophecy.

Born from the first dream cast by the universe itself, Baldri walked the stars before the earth was shaped. He taught mortals the secrets of rest, imagination, and magic. He also placed safeguards between the mortal mind and the horrors of the void. Though once a key figure among the gods, Baldri slowly faded from Pantheon politics, preferring his dreams to their wars and pride.
